# Plan: visual parity report generated by every parity run

**Goal.** Every `npm run parity` (scripts/parity-compare.mjs) run ends by writing a
polished, self-contained visual report of pixel parity across all fixtures —
`parity/out/report.html` (interactive) plus `parity/out/report.png` (shareable
screenshot) — and appends the run to a history file so the report also shows the
trend across runs. It must look great with zero manual steps, every time, for any
number of fixtures/pages.

**Executor notes.** Everything lives in `scripts/parity-compare.mjs` (plain node,
ESM) plus one new template string / small helper module. No new npm deps: the
script already drives Playwright (use the already-launched browser to screenshot
the report), and the chart is plain inline HTML/SVG/CSS — no chart library.
`parity/` is gitignored, so all outputs are local artifacts; the *script* changes
are what get committed.

---

## 1. Data collection (small refactor of parity-compare.mjs)

Today the script prints `${name} page N: X% pixels differ` and writes the
side-by-side PNGs. Change it to accumulate results instead of only printing:

```js
const results = []; // { fixture, page, mismatchPct: number, pngRel: "pickett-p1.png" }
```

After the fixture loop:

1. Write `parity/out/results.json`:
   `{ generatedAt, gitSha, base, results }`
   (`gitSha` via `execFileSync("git", ["rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"])`, allow
   failure → `null`. Do NOT call `Date.now()` anywhere that ends up in the chart's
   deterministic layout — timestamps are data, not layout.)
2. Append one line to `parity/history.jsonl` (create if missing):
   `{ ts, gitSha, results: [{ fixture, page, mismatchPct }] }`
   **Only append when the run covered every reference** (no fixture-name args
   filtering), so partial runs don't pollute the trend. A partial run still
   regenerates the report, using the last full-run history entry for deltas.
3. Generate `report.html` + screenshot `report.png` (sections below).

Also fix the known argv bug while in there: with no `--base` flag,
`baseIdx + 1 === 0` swallows `argv[0]` (the first fixture name). Guard with
`baseIdx >= 0 && i === baseIdx + 1`.

## 2. Report design (already decided — implement as specified)

The report is one static HTML file, inline `<style>` and `<script>`, no external
requests. Root element `class="viz-root"`. Typeface: `system-ui`. Layout: max
width 1100px, centered, 24px paddings; title header "DocxInWeb pixel parity" +
subtitle line with git sha, timestamp, page count.

### 2a. KPI row — four stat tiles (not a chart)

| Tile | Value | Delta shown vs previous full run |
|---|---|---|
| Mean mismatch | mean of all pages, e.g. `4.31%` | ▲/▼ pp diff, green ▼ / red ▲ |
| Worst page | `benchmark p3 — 10.65%` | same |
| Pages ≤ 1% | `9 / 45` | count diff |
| Fixtures | `17` | — |

Hero numbers ≥ 40px, label small caps above, delta line under. Deltas use the
status palette **with a triangle glyph + text**, never color alone. When there is
no history yet, render the tiles without delta lines (no placeholder dashes).

### 2b. Main chart — horizontal bars, one row per fixture-page

The job is "compare magnitude, low→high, across ~45 named items" → horizontal
bars, sorted worst-first, value = mismatch % of that page.

- Row label left: `fixture · pN` (text-secondary; fixture name in text-primary).
- Bar: **single hue** `--series-1` (#2a78d6 light / #3987e5 dark), 14px tall,
  4px rounded *data end only* (baseline end square), baseline at 0 — never
  truncate the axis. X max = `max(5, ceil(dataMax))` percent so tiny values stay
  visible but scale is honest.
- Previous full run's value for the same page: a 2px × 18px **gray tick**
  (`--text-secondary` at 45% opacity) at its x position on the same row — an
  implicit dumbbell showing drift without a second bar. Legend chip explains it
  ("previous run"). Skip when no history.
- Direct label: the value (`3.44%`) right of the bar end in `--text-primary`,
  11px. Every row is labeled — this chart IS the table of record, so labels are
  mandatory, and axis gridlines (every 1% or 2.5%) stay recessive (1px,
  `--grid`, no dark verticals).
- **Hover**: row highlight + tooltip (fixture, page, value, Δ vs previous, "click
  to open diff"). **Click**: `window.open("<fixture>-p<N>.png")` — the
  side-by-side/diff PNG sits in the same directory, so a relative href works.
  This is the feature that makes the report a debugging tool, not a poster.
- Group visually by fixture: 12px gap between fixtures, 4px between pages of the
  same fixture (2px surface gap rule satisfied by row spacing).

### 2c. Trend chart — mismatch over runs (only when history ≥ 2 entries)

Line chart, x = run index (label: short sha), y = mismatch %:
- `mean` in `--series-1` blue, 2px line, ≥8px hover markers;
- `worst page` in `--series-2` aqua (#1baf7a / #199e70), 2px.
Two series → legend present + direct labels at line ends. Crosshair + shared
tooltip listing both values and the sha. Cap at the last 30 runs. Height ~180px.

### 2d. Table view (accessibility non-negotiable)

`<details>` "All pages as text" containing a plain table: fixture, page,
mismatch %, Δ vs previous, link to PNG. This is the color-independent fallback.

### 2e. Theme

CSS custom properties on `.viz-root`, dark values under
`@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` — use exactly these validated values:

```css
.viz-root {
  --surface-1:#fcfcfb; --text-primary:#0b0b0b; --text-secondary:#52514e;
  --grid:#e8e7e3; --series-1:#2a78d6; --series-2:#1baf7a;
  --good:#0ca30c; --critical:#d03b3b;
}
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
  .viz-root {
    --surface-1:#1a1a19; --text-primary:#ffffff; --text-secondary:#c3c2b7;
    --grid:#33332f; --series-1:#3987e5; --series-2:#199e70;
    --good:#0ca30c; --critical:#d03b3b;
  }
}
```

These come pre-validated from the dataviz reference palette (2 series, adjacent
CVD ΔE 24.2 light / 10.3 dark band with direct labels present — which they are,
every bar and line end is labeled). Do not add more hues; there is no third
series. Status colors appear only in KPI deltas and the table's Δ column, always
beside a glyph.

### 2f. PNG export

After writing `report.html`, load it in the already-open Playwright browser
(`file://` URL), viewport 1200×(auto), `colorScheme: "light"`,
`fullPage: true` screenshot → `parity/out/report.png`. Print the two output
paths at the end of the run.

## 3. Implementation steps

1. Refactor the per-page loop to push into `results` (keep the console line).
2. Fix the argv filter bug (§1).
3. Add `buildReport(results, history, meta)` → returns the full HTML string.
   Pure function of its inputs; put it in `scripts/parity-report.mjs` and import
   it, keeping parity-compare.mjs readable. Escape fixture names into HTML.
4. Bars/lines are hand-written SVG with computed viewBox — no libraries. Widths
   from percentages of the x-scale; y positions computed from row index. All
   text in HTML (absolutely positioned divs) or SVG `<text>` — either is fine,
   pick one and be consistent.
5. History read/append with graceful handling: missing file, corrupt lines
   (skip), partial runs (report-only, no append).
6. Screenshot step (§2f) guarded by try/catch — a failed screenshot must not
   fail the parity run.

## 4. Verification (do all before calling it done)

1. `node scripts/parity-compare.mjs --base http://localhost:5299` (full run,
   dev server up; see repo docs for starting it on :5299) → confirm
   `results.json`, `history.jsonl` (+1 line), `report.html`, `report.png`.
2. `node scripts/parity-compare.mjs pickett --base http://localhost:5299` →
   report regenerates, history does NOT grow, first-arg bug confirmed fixed
   (pickett actually runs).
3. Open `report.html` in the browser (light AND dark via DevTools emulation):
   - no label collisions at the real data (45 rows, values 0.25–10.65%);
   - bars sorted worst-first, grouped by fixture;
   - clicking a row opens the right diff PNG;
   - tooltips follow the cursor and never clip at the viewport edge;
   - with `history.jsonl` deleted: no trend section, no deltas, no broken layout.
4. Run twice in a row → trend chart appears with 2 points; deltas render.
5. Look at `report.png` — it must be the light-mode report, full page, legible.
6. Check against the dataviz anti-patterns: one axis only; baseline at zero;
   no rainbow; no color-only meaning; recessive grid; thin marks.

## 5. Out of scope

- CI wiring, committing report artifacts (parity/ stays gitignored).
- Per-pixel-region breakdowns or image diffing changes.
- Line-break-harness (compare-linebreaks.mjs) results — pixel parity only, for
  now; leave a `// TODO` hook where a second results source could merge in.
